REQUIREMENTS","groupId":"a5e46f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p><strong>The major in Chemical Biology is offered by the College of Chemistry</strong>.</p><p><br></p><p>The major provides an understanding of the chemical principles of biological function by emphasizing the development of a solid background in chemistry. In addition to an introductory set of math and physics courses and a broad selection of the same chemistry courses required for the chemistry major, students pursuing the chemical biology major take general and cell biology, biochemistry, biological macromolecular synthesis, and bioinorganic chemistry. The curriculum highlights organic chemistry, quantitative thermodynamics, and kinetics, subjects necessary for understanding the logic of biological systems. The Chemical Biology major is intended for students who are interested in careers as professional chemists, or in the biological sciences including the biomedical, biotechnology, and pharmaceutical industries.</p><p><br></p><p>Please note that Chemical Biology is distinct from Biochemistry. Biochemistry at UC Berkeley is an emphasis within the Molecular and Cell Biology major, housed in the College of Letters and Science. For a detailed understanding of distinctions between Chemical Biology and Molecular & Cell Biology, review and compare upper-division course requirements and descriptions for both majors. </p><p><br></p><p><strong>Transfer applicants are expected to complete, at a minimum, coursework</strong></p><p><strong>equivalent to Berkeley's:</strong></p><p><br></p><p>CHEMISTRY 1A + 1AL + 1B + 3A + 3AL + 3B + 3BL               </p><p>MATH 51 + 52 + 53                             </p><p>PHYSICS 7A or 8A </p><p>READING & COMPOSITION A + READING & COMPOSITION B</p><p>                        </p><p><strong>Coursework must be completed by the end of the spring term that precedes fall enrollment at Berkeley.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>IGETC/Cal-GETC is not required. Students who choose to complete the entire IGETC/Cal-GETC pattern by the end of the spring term preceding fall enrollment at Berkeley may use IGETC/Cal-GETC to fulfill the Reading and Composition and Language Other Than English (LOTE) Requirements.</p><p><br></p><p>Lower division courses required for graduation (but not for admission) are also listed in this articulation agreement. Completion of those courses is strongly recommended in order to strengthen one's application. All major courses must be taken for a letter grade. High grades in major courses (B and A grades exclusively) are essential for applicants to be both competitive in the admissions process and to be adequately prepared to continue with junior year coursework at Berkeley.</p><p><br></p><p>The applicant's personal insight questions (PIQs) are important in the admissions process. The PIQs are reviewed for evidence of the student's interest in the chosen field and a thoughtful match between the intended major and academic and career objectives.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>For more information on College of Chemistry policies and degree programs: </strong></p><p><a href=\"https://chemistry.berkeley.edu/ugrad/degrees\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" Chemical Biology B.S. degree. </p><p><br></p><p><strong>Completion of Chem 3A + 3AL + 3B + 3BL, with a mean grade of B or better, will constitute satisfactory completion of Berkeley's Chem 12A +
